article_meta.title: Former President Donald Trump speaks at a campaign rally on April 27, 2023, in Manchester, New Hampshire

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language and does not adopt CNN Opinion’s more loaded framing (e.g., avoids terms like “despot,” “fabrication,” or overt skepticism language). It attributes findings to CNN’s investigation and experts. Main issue is incomplete sources_represented, not framing inheritance.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like “claimed,” “found,” “consulted”; only mild phrasing like “only remotely similar.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are attributed to CNN’s investigation and cited experts; no unsupported assertions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Avoids declaring Trump’s claims false; sticks to reported lack of evidence.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of intent or beliefs.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes CNN’s fact-check findings, reflecting the article’s focus; minimal caveat about uncertainty omitted.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Sources_represented lists only CNN, omitting Trump/campaign, anti- and pro-immigration orgs mentioned.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Does not overemphasize negative framings beyond article content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats multiple source types similarly; no clear deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source, avoiding its loaded descriptors.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Major perspectives present in the article (Trump/campaign, anti-immigration groups, pro-immigration experts) are not reflected in sources_represented.
